meaning_english: To support someone or to make a copy of computer data. Synonyms: Support, Corroborate, Duplicate.
To cause something to happen. Synonyms: Cause, Produce, Induce.
To require or demand something. Synonyms: Require, Demand, Necessity.
To complete a task or to follow instructions. Synonyms: Execute, Perform, Implement.
To reach the same quality or standard as someone else. Synonyms: Reach, Overtake.
To obtain something that is hard to get. Synonyms: Obtain, Acquire, Get.
To be suited for a particular profession or activity. Synonyms: Suited, Qualified, Fit.
To get rid of something or abolish it. Synonyms: Abolish, Eliminate, Discard.
To fail to happen (usually plans or deals). Synonyms: Fail, Collapse, Miscarry.
To understand or find a solution to a problem. Synonyms: Understand, Solve, Work out.
To manage to live or survive with very little money or resources. Synonyms: Manage, Survive, Subsist.
To recover from an illness or an emotional setback. Synonyms: Recover, Overcome.
To finally agree to something or to surrender. Synonyms: Yield, Surrender, Capitulate.
To examine or review something carefully. Synonyms: Review, Examine, Check.
To continue doing something. Synonyms: Continue, Persist.
To dismiss employees from their jobs (usually due to lack of work). Synonyms: Dismiss, Fire, Redundant.
To think that someone is less important or inferior. Synonyms: Despise, Scorn, Disdain.
To admire or respect someone greatly. Synonyms: Admire, Respect, Revere.
To manage to see, hear, or understand something with difficulty. Synonyms: Discern, Distinguish, Understand.
To compensate for a mistake or a loss. Synonyms: Compensate, Atone, Offset.
A polite way to say someone has died. Synonyms: Die, Expire.
To survive a dangerous or difficult situation (like an illness). Synonyms: Survive, Recover.
To communicate an idea clearly. Synonyms: Communicate, Convey, Explain.
When something unpleasant starts and seems likely to continue. Synonyms: Begin, Start, Establish.
To support someone or to be ready for action. Synonyms: Support, Wait.
To resemble an older relative in appearance or character. Synonyms: Resemble, Look like.
To accept a responsibility or to challenge an opponent. Synonyms: Undertake, Challenge.
To result in a particular way or to be discovered as. Synonyms: Result, Prove to be.
To serve someone, especially in a restaurant. Synonyms: Serve, Attend to.
To solve a problem or to do physical exercise. Synonyms: Solve, Exercise, Resolve.
